one_line_definition: Stiffness per unit area of a partially-contacting interface, defined as dP_nom/du; the key parameter extracted from ultrasonic reflection coefficients
Ratio of reflected to incident ultrasonic wave amplitude at an interface; depends on interfacial stiffness K and frequency ω
Long-wavelength approximation treating a partially-contacting interface as a distributed elastic spring layer characterised by stiffness K per unit area

aliases_seen: RCA, A/A₀, fraction of real contact area
one_line_definition: Actual fraction of nominal contact area where asperities are in solid–solid contact; much less than apparent area for rough surfaces
Rate of change of contact pressure with interfacial approach displacement; measurable from ultrasonic multi-frequency transmission data
Damping parameter in the acoustic contact model representing viscous energy dissipation at asperity micro-contacts; related to real contact area
Energy loss mechanism in which oscillating contact asperities radiate acoustic energy back into the surrounding medium
Asymptotic statistical rough-surface contact model treating asperities as random elliptical paraboloids; best overall match to ultrasonic measurements among classical models
Statistical elastic contact model assuming Gaussian distribution of hemisphere-capped asperities with uniform radius; tends to underpredict stiffness and contact area for real surfaces
Product of material density and acoustic wave velocity (Z = ρv); mismatch across an interface determines baseline reflection coefficient
Ultrasonic technique measuring round-trip travel time to determine material thickness or wear depth
Frequency-domain technique using destructive interference dips to measure thin material thickness when echoes overlap in time domain
Optical technique using birefringent material to visualise internal stress distribution under load
Elastic contact theory predicting F ∝ δ^(3/2) for spheres and a logarithmic F-δ relation for cylinders
Sound wave speed in a 1D granular chain depends on confining force via power law; exponent β relates to contact law nonlinearity
Large-amplitude nonlinear solitary wave propagating in 1D bead chains without a prestress, predicted by Nesterenko (1983)
Increase in true contact area of ductile metals during sliding due to adhesive junctions growing under combined normal and tangential stresses
Method of applying contact pressure uniformly using a pressurised fluid enclosure, enabling study of very high contact pressures without specimen damage
Phase angle of the complex reflection coefficient at a contact interface; provides a functional relationship between K and A/A₀ but cannot separate them independently
Fractional area of a surface cross-section at a given height; used as a proxy for real contact area after plastic deformation
Ultrasonic waves with particle motion perpendicular to propagation direction; less sensitive to applied normal stress, used as alternative to longitudinal waves for RCA measurement
Pre-contact measurement of full reflection from a solid-air interface used as the 100 % baseline for computing reflection coefficient R
Process by which repeated loading progressively eliminates plastic deformation until contact becomes nominally elastic; identified from hysteresis in R-load cycles
Empirical linear relationship between interfacial stiffness K and contact pressure P_nom, obtained from a known-geometry loading experiment on the same material pair
Statistical descriptors of a surface height profile used to characterise asperity density, radius, and height distribution in contact models
